dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won a light but favorable day on comparative usefulness: commenters said ChatGPT/GPT 5.2 outperformed a retreat-planning agent, and another user praised o3-thinking with web search as a faster, good-enough replacement for Deep Research in real use [E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI edged the day because its clearest direct mentions were about practical product preference rather than bugs or broken commitments. The strongest signals were a favorable GPT/ChatGPT comparison in the TeamOut launch thread and praise for the speed-versus-thoroughness trade-off of o3-thinking with web search in the Mercury discussion [E1][E2]. The win was narrow, not dominant, because coverage was limited and there was still a smaller responsibility-related knock in a separate safety discussion [E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: Comparative product praise drove the day: in the TeamOut launch thread, commenters said GPT 5.2/ChatGPT produced a much better researched answer than the featured agent, and in the Mercury thread one user said o3-thinking with web search displaced Deep Research because it kept most of the value with far less waiting [E1][E2]. A smaller trust hit came from a safety discussion where OpenAI was contrasted unfavorably on responsibility [E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Two direct hits dominated: commenters blasted Anthropic for dropping its safety pledge and called Claude Code Remote Control a clunky, buggy prerelease; separate complaints about Claude wasting context in tool-heavy workflows added more drag [E4][E5][E7]. Praise for Claude Code's searchable tool use and one report that Claude fixed real Java interop issues softened but did not erase the damage [E6][E8].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The meaningful mentions were both about added baggage rather than usefulness: one MCP thread cited the GitHub Copilot VS Code extension as loading many tools and bloating context, while Notepad commenters mocked Copilot being shoved into a simple text editor [E9][E10].

evidence.summary: In the TeamOut launch thread, top feedback said the product's AI answer was worse than GPT 5.2 and that ChatGPT gave a more well-researched response.
In the Mercury 2 thread, a commenter said they switched from OpenAI Deep Research to o3-thinking with web search because it delivered most of the thoroughness in much less time.
In discussion of Anthropic's safety pledge, one commenter contrasted OpenAI unfavorably on responsibility and said the company had largely convinced them it was not seriously trying to maintain it.
The safety-pledge thread drew strong criticism that Anthropic abandoned a key precommitment and looked driven by competitive pressure rather than principles.
Claude Code Remote Control was described as an extremely clunky, buggy prerelease with stop failures, disconnects, and poor introspection.
In the MCP thread, commenters said Claude Code can search for tools without dumping everything into context and cited Anthropic's tool-use approach as addressing MCP bloat.
A workflow thread complained that Claude repeatedly ran noisy commands, reread huge outputs, and polluted context badly enough that the user banned it from direct gradle use.
In the cl-kawa discussion, the project author said Claude fixed remaining issues blocking Kawa and upgraded the Java support from 8 to 21.
The MCP discussion cited the GitHub Copilot VS Code extension as exposing roughly 20 tools, contributing to context bloat and extra baggage.
Notepad commenters mocked Microsoft for putting Copilot into a formerly simple editor and treated the AI tie-in as unnecessary clutter.
